chief_complaint: A 40-year-old man presented with severe abdominal pain that had progressed over 2 days.
history: A man in his 40s of Middle Eastern descent presented with severe abdominal pain that had progressed over 2 days. He was diagnosed with acute necrotising pancreatitis, multifocal venous thromboembolism and acute kidney injury, requiring admission to the intensive care unit. After exclusion of common causes of pancreatitis, a systemic inflammatory or vasculitic process was suspected.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not explicitly stated.
labs: ECG, bloods, imaging, and other tests not explicitly stated.
correct_diagnosis: Acute necrotising pancreatitis in a patient with Behcet's disease
differentials: Acute necrotising pancreatitis in a patient with Behcet's disease
Acute necrotising pancreatitis due to gallstones
Acute necrotising pancreatitis due to alcohol abuse
correct_ranking: Acute necrotising pancreatitis in a patient with Behcet's disease
